warm: We sound like a friend who knows the answer. Approachable, plain spoken, never preachy. We celebrate small wins with our community and treat every question like it matters. Our copy reads at an 8th grade level so anyone can engage without feeling talked down to.
clinical: Precise, evidence based, and trustworthy. We cite facts and avoid hyperbole. Our tone is calm and informed, like a doctor who actually listens. Never alarmist, never salesy. We let credibility do the talking.
edgy: Direct, a little irreverent, and unafraid to call out industry nonsense. We use short sentences. We make our point and move on. Humor is welcome but never at the customer's expense. We respect smart readers and write to them, not down at them.
casual: Conversational and easy. Contractions, sentence fragments where they make a point, and occasional asides in parentheses (because that is how people talk). We sound like the most knowledgeable person at the cookout, not at the conference.
professional: Polished, confident, and informed. We choose our words deliberately and avoid filler. Our voice projects expertise without being stuffy. Industry terms get used when accuracy matters and explained when it does not.
playful: Light hearted, occasionally cheeky, and always optimistic. We find moments of joy in the everyday. We use exclamation points sparingly so they actually mean something when they land.
authoritative: Confident leadership voice grounded in years of experience. We make recommendations clearly and stand behind them. We acknowledge complexity but do not hide behind it. Our customers come to us because we know our craft.
empathetic: We meet people where they are. Big life moments, hard decisions, frustrating problems, we treat each one with patience and respect. We never rush, never minimize, never make people feel small. We listen first.
